---
title: "Population PK Portfolio — Theophylline"
subtitle: "A complete, reproducible end-to-end pharmacokinetics workflow in R"
author: "Pramod BR"
date: today
---

## Overview

This portfolio demonstrates a complete **Population Pharmacokinetics (PopPK)** workflow on the
classic **Theophylline** dataset — from exploratory analysis through noncompartmental analysis,
nonlinear mixed-effects modelling, covariate evaluation, diagnostics, and clinical simulation.
It was built to showcase practical skills in clinical pharmacology, DMPK, and pharmacometrics.

## Drug context

Theophylline is a **narrow-therapeutic-index** bronchodilator with well-characterised
one-compartment oral pharmacokinetics. The built-in `Theoph` dataset contains rich serial
sampling from **12 subjects** after a single oral dose, with **body weight** recorded as a
covariate of interest — making it an ideal teaching dataset for population PK methodology.

## Analysis phases

| Phase | Report | Key skills |
|------:|--------|------------|
| 1 | [Exploratory Data Analysis](reports/01_EDA_Report.html) | Data QC, PK visualisation, study design |
| 2 | [Noncompartmental Analysis](reports/02_NCA_Report.html) | PKNCA — Cmax, Tmax, AUC, t½ |
| 3 | [Base PopPK Model](reports/03_Base_Model_Report.html) | nlmixr2 / rxode2, 1-cmt oral, first-order absorption |
| 4 | [IIV Model](reports/04_IIV_Model_Report.html) | Between-subject variability on CL, V, Ka |
| 5 | [Covariate Model](reports/05_Covariate_Model_Report.html) | Weight on CL & V, model selection |
| 6 | [Model Diagnostics](reports/06_Model_Diagnostics.html) | GOF, VPC, CWRES vs TIME/PRED |
| 7 | [Clinical Simulation](reports/07_Simulation_Report.html) | Typical-patient & subgroup PK simulation |
| 9 | [1- vs 2-Compartment](reports/08_Two_Compartment_Report.html) | Structural model selection (OFV/AIC/BIC) |
| 10 | [Bootstrap CIs](reports/09_Bootstrap_Report.html) | Empirical parameter uncertainty |
| 11 | [Covariate Forest Plot](reports/10_Forest_Plot_Report.html) | Clinical relevance of the weight effect |

## Reproducibility

The project uses **`renv`** for a locked package environment, **`here`** for path-safe code,
and **Quarto** to render every analysis to this website (published from the `docs/` folder via
GitHub Pages). Each phase is a standalone, re-runnable script in `R/` paired with a report in
`reports/`.

---

*Disclaimer: built with publicly available data for educational and portfolio purposes only;
not intended to guide clinical dosing.*
